title: centaurea irritans var. irritans
intro: Centaurea irritans var. irritans, also known as the St. Anthony's Spur, is a biennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its thistle-like appearance and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of essential oils and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Centaurea irritans var. irritans include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and combat bacterial infections. Herbal preparations of Centaurea irritans var. irritans include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
